object: ordinary Arcane Lamp (blockMetalDevice metadata 7) including its invisible blockAiry metadata-3 marker and direct Arcane Bore/Bore Base integrations
proved_discrepancies: The port required a sturdy support face while TC4 only removed the lamp when the support block became air.

The port bound a ticking BlockEntity to invisible light markers, stored source coordinates and self-removed orphans; TC4 TileArcaneLampLight exists in source but is never registered or bound to blockAiry metadata 3.
The port clamped candidates to maxBuildHeight-1 and skipped unloaded chunks; TC4 only capped to surface+4 and minimum Y 5, then used direct world access.
The port omitted the Arcane Bore post-mining light-marker integration.

The block model rotated the fixed body and approximated the Bore nozzle with lamp textures; TC4 keeps the body fixed and renders ModelBoreBase.renderNozzle with Bore.png, including a second connector on Arcane Bore Base.
The arcane recipe lost spaces and mapped glowstone/nitor/arcane stone incorrectly.
The item had a non-original uncommon rarity.

resource_evidence.lamp_side: byte-identical to original TC4 lamp_side.png and mcmeta
resource_evidence.lamp_top: byte-identical to original TC4 lamp_top.png and mcmeta
resource_evidence.item_model: body only, matching original inventory renderer

build_blocker: Gradle Wrapper cannot download Gradle 7.5.1 because services.gradle.org is unavailable in the execution environment; installed runtime is Java 21 rather than the required Java 17.
honesty: No BUILD VERIFIED or RUNTIME VERIFIED claim is made.
